description_long: This is a large, two-handed sword with a straight, double-edged blade that tapers to a point. The blade is long and robust, with a subtle central ridge running down its length, suggesting strength and durability. The crossguard is gently curved and extends outward, providing hand protection, while the handle is cylindrical and appears to be made of the same dark metal as the blade. The pommel is spherical, adding balance and a classic medieval aesthetic. The entire sword has a matte, dark metallic finish with no visible ornamentation or embellishments. Its proportions and design evoke the look of a European longsword, suitable for display or use in historical reenactments.
description: A large, straight-bladed, double-edged sword with a curved crossguard and spherical pommel, all in a dark matte metal finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the straight blade, curved crossguard, and round pommel.
description_view_1: Side profile revealing the thinness of the blade and handle, with the crossguard and pommel visible as narrow lines.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the blade, crossguard, and pommel from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again highlighting the slimness of the sword and the alignment of the handle and blade.
attribute_handle_design: straight cylindrical metal handle with spherical pommel, no visible grip texture
